priority_intro: For each topic below, you will first be asked how important this issue is to you personally, then answer 2-3 questions about your position. Your responses help match you with candidates whose views align with yours. There are no right or wrong answers — this quiz is designed to reflect your genuine views, not to judge them.
quiz_intro: This questionnaire helps Flower Mound voters understand where they stand on key issues ahead of the November 2026 elections — covering local, state, and federal races. For each topic, rate how important the issue is to you, then answer a few questions about your position using a 1-to-5 scale. A score of 3 always means you have no strong opinion or prefer a middle-ground approach. All questions are designed to be fair to voters across the political spectrum. Your results will be compared against candidate responses to show you who aligns most closely with your views.

topics.priority_question: How important is fiscal policy and taxation to you when deciding how to vote?

questions.question_text: When it comes to government spending and taxation, which approach do you prefer?
questions.scale_low_label: Government should significantly reduce spending and lower taxes, even if some services are cut
questions.scale_high_label: Government should expand services and invest more in public programs, even if taxes increase

questions.question_text: Property taxes are the primary way Texas funds schools and local services. How should the state balance property tax relief against funding for those services?
questions.scale_low_label: Prioritize capping and cutting property taxes — homeowners are overburdened, and governments should live within tighter budgets
questions.scale_high_label: Prioritize stable funding for schools and local services — property tax relief should not come at their expense
questions.neutrality_notes: Both poles framed around priorities rather than outcomes; avoids asserting whether cuts do or do not reduce service quality, which is itself disputed.

questions.question_text: When the government faces a budget deficit, what is the preferred response?
questions.scale_low_label: Cut spending to balance the budget, even if programs are reduced
questions.scale_high_label: Raise revenue through new or higher taxes before cutting programs

topics.priority_question: How important are public safety and criminal justice issues to you when deciding how to vote?

questions.question_text: How should communities approach funding and staffing for local law enforcement?
questions.scale_low_label: Significantly reduce law enforcement budgets and redirect funds to social services
questions.scale_high_label: Significantly increase law enforcement funding, staffing, and equipment

questions.question_text: When someone is convicted of a crime, what should the primary goal of their sentence be?
questions.scale_low_label: Rehabilitation and reintegration into society
questions.scale_high_label: Punishment and deterrence through strict sentencing

questions.question_text: How should the criminal justice system handle nonviolent drug offenses?
questions.scale_low_label: Treat them primarily as a public health matter — focus on treatment over prosecution
questions.scale_high_label: Treat them primarily as a legal matter — enforce existing laws consistently

topics.priority_question: How important is education funding and access to you when deciding how to vote?

questions.question_text: Should Texas increase per-student funding for public schools, even if it requires higher state spending?
questions.scale_low_label: No — public schools should improve efficiency within current funding levels
questions.scale_high_label: Yes — Texas should significantly increase per-student public school funding
questions.neutrality_notes: Replaces a federal-vs-local control question that measured federalism rather than education positions.

questions.question_text: Should public funding be available for families to use at private, charter, or home-school settings?
questions.scale_low_label: Public education funding should be directed exclusively to traditional public schools
questions.scale_high_label: Families should be able to use public education funds at any accredited school, including private or charter options

questions.question_text: How should the cost of higher education (college and vocational programs) be addressed?
questions.scale_low_label: Tuition costs are primarily the responsibility of students and families; government subsidies should be limited
questions.scale_high_label: Government should substantially subsidize or forgive higher education costs to expand access

topics.priority_question: How important are reproductive rights and related policies to you when deciding how to vote?

questions.question_text: How should abortion access be regulated by law?
questions.scale_low_label: Abortion should be prohibited in most or all circumstances
questions.scale_high_label: Abortion should be legal and accessible without significant restrictions
questions.neutrality_notes: This topic has no framing that will feel fully neutral to all voters. The scale endpoints describe positions held by real voters and avoid loaded language like 'pro-life' or 'pro-choice.' A score of 3 captures the large share of voters who support limited access with some exceptions.

questions.question_text: Should government funding (such as Medicaid) cover abortion services?
questions.scale_low_label: No — public funds should not be used to pay for abortion procedures
questions.scale_high_label: Yes — abortion should be covered like other medical procedures in public health programs
questions.neutrality_notes: Framed around funding policy rather than moral judgment to keep the question procedural.

questions.question_text: How should laws address access to contraception?
questions.scale_low_label: Access to contraception should be subject to greater legal restrictions or employer/insurer exemptions
questions.scale_high_label: Access to contraception should be guaranteed and covered without restriction
questions.neutrality_notes: Difficult to word neutrally because the two sides disagree on whether this is a settled issue. Framed around legal access rather than personal morality.

topics.priority_question: How important are social safety net programs (welfare, food assistance, housing assistance) to you when deciding how to vote?

questions.question_text: What role should government play in providing assistance to low-income individuals and families?
questions.scale_low_label: Government assistance should be minimal — private charities and community organizations are better suited to help those in need
questions.scale_high_label: Government should provide robust, broad assistance programs to ensure a baseline standard of living for all residents

questions.question_text: Should receiving government assistance benefits be tied to work or job-search requirements?
questions.scale_low_label: Work requirements should be strictly enforced for most able-bodied adults receiving assistance
questions.scale_high_label: Benefits should be provided without work requirements to avoid penalizing people facing barriers to employment

questions.question_text: How should government address housing affordability for lower-income residents?
questions.scale_low_label: Market forces should determine housing availability and pricing with minimal government intervention
questions.scale_high_label: Government should actively subsidize affordable housing construction and provide rental assistance programs

topics.priority_question: How important are Social Security and Medicare to you when deciding how to vote?

questions.question_text: How should the long-term funding shortfall in Social Security be addressed?
questions.scale_low_label: Raise the retirement age and reduce benefit growth to keep the program financially sustainable without tax increases
questions.scale_high_label: Increase payroll taxes or remove income caps on contributions to preserve or expand current benefits

questions.question_text: How should Medicare costs and coverage be managed going forward?
questions.scale_low_label: Introduce more market competition and private options to control Medicare costs
questions.scale_high_label: Expand Medicare coverage and increase government negotiating power to control costs

questions.question_text: Should individuals be allowed to invest a portion of their Social Security contributions in private accounts?
questions.scale_low_label: No — Social Security should remain a guaranteed benefit administered entirely by the government
questions.scale_high_label: Yes — workers should have the option to direct some contributions into personal investment accounts

topics.priority_question: How important are LGBTQ+ rights and related policies to you when deciding how to vote?

questions.question_text: Should federal and state laws explicitly prohibit discrimination against LGBTQ+ individuals in employment, housing, and public accommodations?
questions.scale_low_label: Existing laws are sufficient; additional explicit LGBTQ+ protections are not needed
questions.scale_high_label: Explicit legal protections for LGBTQ+ individuals should be added or strengthened at the federal and state level
questions.neutrality_notes: Framed around legal protection policy rather than personal or religious views. This remains difficult to word without implicitly validating one side's framing.

questions.question_text: How should laws address gender-affirming medical care for minors?
questions.scale_low_label: Gender-affirming medical treatments for minors should be prohibited or heavily restricted by law
questions.scale_high_label: Decisions about gender-affirming care for minors should be left to patients, families, and medical providers without legal prohibition
questions.neutrality_notes: This is one of the most difficult questions to frame neutrally. Both endpoints were worded to describe a legal policy stance without using charged advocacy language.

questions.question_text: Should religious organizations and private businesses be permitted to decline services based on sincere religious beliefs about marriage or gender?
questions.scale_low_label: No — anti-discrimination laws should apply to all businesses and organizations regardless of religious belief
questions.scale_high_label: Yes — religious liberty protections should allow individuals and organizations to decline participation in activities that conflict with their beliefs
questions.neutrality_notes: Difficult to word without implying a hierarchy between competing rights. The framing attempts to present both as principled legal positions.

topics.priority_question: How important is healthcare access and affordability to you when deciding how to vote?

questions.question_text: How should health insurance coverage for Americans be primarily structured?
questions.scale_low_label: Through private markets and employer-based plans with limited government involvement
questions.scale_high_label: Through a government-administered program that covers all residents, replacing or supplementing private insurance

questions.question_text: Should the government be allowed to negotiate prescription drug prices directly with pharmaceutical companies?
questions.scale_low_label: No — price negotiation by government distorts the market and may reduce innovation
questions.scale_high_label: Yes — government negotiation would lower costs for patients and should be expanded

questions.question_text: Texas has so far declined to expand Medicaid eligibility under the Affordable Care Act. Should Texas expand Medicaid to cover more low-income adults?
questions.scale_low_label: No — Texas should not expand Medicaid; it would grow government health spending and dependence
questions.scale_high_label: Yes — Texas should expand Medicaid to cover more low-income adults
questions.neutrality_notes: Reframed as the state-level decision actually before the legislature, removing the federal-mandate confound.

topics.priority_question: How important is immigration policy to you when deciding how to vote?

questions.question_text: How should the United States handle enforcement at the southern border?
questions.scale_low_label: Significantly increase physical barriers, personnel, and legal consequences to reduce unauthorized crossings
questions.scale_high_label: Focus resources on humanitarian processing and legal pathways rather than expanded enforcement measures
questions.neutrality_notes: Framed around policy mechanisms rather than characterizing migrants. 'Unauthorized' is used as a neutral legal descriptor rather than 'illegal' or 'undocumented,' which carry different connotations for different audiences.

questions.question_text: How should the U.S. address the status of people currently living in the country without legal authorization?
questions.scale_low_label: Prioritize identification and removal of those without legal status, with limited pathways to legal residency
questions.scale_high_label: Create broad pathways to legal status or citizenship for long-term residents, regardless of how they arrived
questions.neutrality_notes: Intentionally avoids the word 'amnesty' (perceived as charged by one side) and 'deportation' (perceived as harsh by the other). Describes the policy outcome instead.

questions.question_text: How should annual levels of legal immigration to the United States be adjusted?
questions.scale_low_label: Legal immigration levels should be significantly reduced
questions.scale_high_label: Legal immigration levels should be significantly increased

topics.priority_question: How important is gun policy to you when deciding how to vote?

questions.question_text: How should background check requirements for firearm purchases be structured?
questions.scale_low_label: Current background check requirements are sufficient or should be reduced
questions.scale_high_label: Background checks should be expanded to cover all firearm sales, including private transfers and gun shows

questions.question_text: How should laws treat the ownership of semi-automatic rifles with detachable magazines (sometimes called 'assault-style' weapons)?
questions.scale_low_label: These firearms should be legal to own with no additional restrictions beyond standard gun laws
questions.scale_high_label: These firearms should be subject to significant additional restrictions or bans on civilian ownership
questions.neutrality_notes: The term 'assault weapon' is avoided as it is disputed. The question uses a descriptive technical definition instead.

questions.question_text: Should states allow individuals to carry a concealed handgun in public without a government-issued permit?
questions.scale_low_label: No — a permit process with training requirements should be required to carry a concealed firearm
questions.scale_high_label: Yes — law-abiding citizens should be able to carry a concealed firearm without needing government permission

topics.priority_question: How important are energy policy and environmental issues to you when deciding how to vote?

questions.question_text: How should the U.S. approach its transition away from fossil fuels toward renewable energy?
questions.scale_low_label: The government should not mandate or heavily subsidize a transition — the energy mix should be driven by market forces
questions.scale_high_label: The government should set aggressive timelines and provide major funding to accelerate a transition to renewable energy

questions.question_text: How strictly should the federal government regulate greenhouse gas emissions from industry and power plants?
questions.scale_low_label: Current regulations are sufficient or go too far — stricter regulations impose excessive costs on businesses and consumers
questions.scale_high_label: Greenhouse gas regulations should be significantly strengthened to reduce emissions, even at higher cost to industry

questions.question_text: Given that Texas is a major energy-producing state, how should state policy balance energy production with environmental protection?
questions.scale_low_label: Prioritize maximizing energy production — oil, gas, and other resources are vital to Texas's economy and should face minimal restriction
questions.scale_high_label: Prioritize stronger environmental safeguards even if they limit some energy production or raise energy costs

topics.priority_question: How important are infrastructure and transportation issues to you when deciding how to vote?

questions.question_text: How should major infrastructure projects (roads, bridges, broadband, water systems) be funded?
questions.scale_low_label: Primarily through user fees, tolls, and private investment rather than broad tax funding
questions.scale_high_label: Primarily through public funding and federal investment, distributed broadly across communities

questions.question_text: Given the growth along the DFW metroplex, how should transportation investment in the Flower Mound and Denton County area be directed?
questions.scale_low_label: Focus almost entirely on road capacity expansion and highway improvements for personal vehicles
questions.scale_high_label: Invest significantly in public transit options (commuter rail, bus rapid transit) alongside road improvements

questions.question_text: Texas faces growing strain on its water supply and electric grid. How should the state respond?
questions.scale_low_label: Rely primarily on market incentives and private investment to expand water and power capacity
questions.scale_high_label: Make major public investments in water infrastructure and grid reliability, even if it requires new state spending or fees
questions.neutrality_notes: Replaces a toll-road question with weak ideological signal; water and grid are top differentiating 2026 issues with researchable candidate positions.

topics.priority_question: How important are land use and development decisions to you when deciding how to vote?

questions.question_text: How should Flower Mound and Denton County approach residential development as the DFW region continues to grow?
questions.scale_low_label: Maintain strong zoning restrictions and slow growth to preserve existing neighborhood character
questions.scale_high_label: Allow significantly more housing development, including higher density, to meet demand and keep housing affordable

questions.question_text: When private development conflicts with preserving green space or open land, which should generally take priority?
questions.scale_low_label: Private development rights and economic growth should generally take priority
questions.scale_high_label: Preservation of open space, trees, and natural land should generally take priority

questions.question_text: Should the state of Texas have the authority to override local zoning decisions to allow more housing?
questions.scale_low_label: No — local communities should retain full control over their own zoning and land use decisions
questions.scale_high_label: Yes — the state should be able to mandate zoning changes in cities and towns to address regional housing needs

topics.priority_question: How important is managing Flower Mound's community character and growth to you when deciding how to vote?

questions.question_text: How should Flower Mound balance attracting new commercial and retail development with preserving its current suburban character?
questions.scale_low_label: Prioritize preserving Flower Mound's existing low-density, residential character over new commercial growth
questions.scale_high_label: Actively attract new commercial development, mixed-use areas, and businesses to diversify the tax base and add amenities

questions.question_text: As Flower Mound's population grows, how should the town respond to demand for more housing?
questions.scale_low_label: Limit further residential development to avoid straining schools, roads, and services
questions.scale_high_label: Accommodate residential growth with expanded infrastructure and allow new neighborhoods to be built

questions.question_text: Who should bear the cost of infrastructure needed to support new development (roads, utilities, schools)?
questions.scale_low_label: Primarily developers and new residents, through impact fees and special assessments on new construction
questions.scale_high_label: Primarily the broader community, through general taxes — keeping fees low to encourage growth and keep housing affordable
questions.neutrality_notes: Impact-fee allocation genuinely divides county and local candidates; neither pole is framed as fiscally reckless.

topics.priority_question: How important are education curriculum decisions and parental rights in schools to you when deciding how to vote?

questions.question_text: How should Lewisville ISD (LISD) and the state approach teaching topics related to race, historical discrimination, and systemic inequality?
questions.scale_low_label: Schools should teach a straightforward factual history without exploring systemic or structural interpretations of race and inequality
questions.scale_high_label: Schools should include examination of how race and systemic inequality have shaped American history and institutions
questions.neutrality_notes: This is among the most difficult questions to phrase neutrally. Both endpoints were constructed to describe an approach to curriculum without using contested terms like 'CRT' or 'whitewashing.' Reasonable voters will still perceive framing differently.

questions.question_text: How much control should parents have over their child's exposure to specific books, materials, or topics in public school classrooms and libraries?
questions.scale_low_label: Curriculum and library decisions should be made primarily by trained educators and school boards, with limited individual parent veto power
questions.scale_high_label: Parents should have strong authority to remove their child from lessons or require removal of materials they find objectionable

questions.question_text: How should public schools handle discussions of gender identity and sexual orientation in age-appropriate settings?
questions.scale_low_label: These topics should not be introduced in K-12 classrooms — they are family and private matters
questions.scale_high_label: Age-appropriate instruction on gender identity and sexual orientation should be included as part of health and social education
questions.neutrality_notes: Difficult to phrase neutrally. The phrase 'age-appropriate' is included in the high-end label to reflect what proponents actually advocate, rather than a strawman. Both endpoints describe a genuine policy position.

topics.priority_question: How important are local government accountability and transparency issues to you when deciding how to vote?

questions.question_text: How far should local government go in publishing its internal records — budgets, contracts, and development negotiations?
questions.scale_low_label: Publish what the law requires — staff time is better spent delivering services, and publishing draft negotiations can hurt the town's bargaining position
questions.scale_high_label: Proactively publish nearly everything, even if it adds administrative cost and slows some negotiations
questions.neutrality_notes: Rewritten with real costs on the transparency side so the question no longer has a universally agreeable answer.

questions.question_text: Should residents have more direct input mechanisms — such as citizen ballot initiatives or binding referendums — on major local decisions?
questions.scale_low_label: Elected officials and professional staff are best suited to make complex local decisions without requiring direct voter approval
questions.scale_high_label: Residents should have more direct mechanisms to approve or reject major decisions, bypassing elected officials when needed

questions.question_text: How strict should ethics rules be for local officials with financial ties to businesses before the town or county?
questions.scale_low_label: Current disclosure and recusal rules are adequate — overly strict rules discourage qualified residents with local business experience from serving
questions.scale_high_label: Much stricter rules — mandatory recusal, divestment, and independent enforcement, even if it shrinks the pool of willing candidates
questions.neutrality_notes: Cost framing added to the strict-ethics pole to break the social-desirability ceiling.

questions.question_text: How should local election administration balance voting access and election security?
questions.scale_low_label: Prioritize security and uniform procedures — tighter ID verification and limited alternative voting methods, even if some voters find it less convenient
questions.scale_high_label: Prioritize access and convenience — expanded early voting, more locations, and easier mail ballots, even if procedures vary more
questions.neutrality_notes: Directly relevant to the county clerk race; both poles framed as legitimate priorities.
